name: Korean fintech product review
summary: Reviews Korean financial product flows for trusted input, identity, payment disclosure, consent, dense data, and accessible responsive behavior.

criteria.title: Give Korean phone fields native input semantics
criteria.question: Can a mobile user enter and autofill a Korean phone number without changing keyboards or guessing the field purpose?
criteria.evidence: Inspect phone-like inputs for type=tel and autocomplete=tel.
verification: Focus every phone field on mobile and confirm the numeric phone keypad and saved-number autofill are available.
falsePositiveNotes: Exclude search, account number, and one-time-code fields even when their placeholder contains digits.

criteria.title: Declare password autofill intent
criteria.question: Can password managers distinguish sign-in credentials from a newly created password?
criteria.evidence: Inspect password inputs for current-password or new-password autocomplete tokens.
verification: Exercise sign-in and password creation with a platform password manager.
falsePositiveNotes: A transaction PIN is not a web account password and may use a different secured input contract.

criteria.title: Keep optional marketing consent unchecked
criteria.question: Is marketing consent separate from required terms and off by default?
criteria.evidence: Inspect checked checkboxes whose associated label mentions marketing, advertising, or promotional messages.
verification: Load a fresh session and confirm optional marketing consent is unchecked and independently operable.
falsePositiveNotes: Do not classify a read-only consent receipt or previously saved account preference as a new opt-in default.

criteria.title: Show the full payment commitment before approval
criteria.question: Before payment, can the user see amount, billing frequency, next charge date, cancellation terms, and the selected payment method?
criteria.evidence: Capture the final confirmation state and the payment completion state with all monetary terms visible.
verification: Review one-time, subscription, failure, retry, and refund paths with Korean copy and amounts.
falsePositiveNotes: A one-time purchase does not need recurring billing fields, but still needs total amount and cancellation or refund terms.

criteria.title: Keep dense financial data scannable
criteria.question: Do aligned numerals, row density, hierarchy, focus, and touch targets remain legible at mobile and desktop widths?
criteria.evidence: Capture account, transaction, and comparison surfaces at both declared viewports with overflow and keyboard focus visible.
verification: Check tabular numerals, 44px touch targets, visible focus, horizontal overflow, and Korean line-height.
falsePositiveNotes: Dense desktop tables may intentionally scroll horizontally on mobile when a card transformation would remove necessary comparison context.

criteria.title: Prove the financial flow with assistive technology
criteria.question: Can keyboard and screen-reader users complete identity, payment, error recovery, and confirmation without losing context?
criteria.evidence: Attach keyboard order, accessibility-tree, error announcement, contrast, and focus-restoration evidence.
verification: Run the approved browser verification profile at mobile and desktop viewports.
falsePositiveNotes: Static accessible names do not prove modal focus management, live announcements, or vendor handoff recovery.
The fixture intentionally contains three static defects; production examples with correct semantics must not emit those confirmed findings.

notes: The pack classifies supplied evidence only. It does not decide legal compliance or execute payment and identity providers.
